input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-04-09T15:21:31.596Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` to specify values for `Light`, `Dark`, and optionally `Default`:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework reads the device theme at startup **and** reacts to live changes — no event wiring needed.

---

**C# equivalent** — use `SetAppTheme` or construct an `AppThemeBinding` object:

```csharp
// Extension method (simplest)
var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

// Or the full binding object
var page = new ContentPage();
page.SetBinding(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    new AppThemeBinding
    {
        Light = Colors.White,
        Dark  = Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E")
    });
```

`SetAppTheme(property, lightValue, darkValue)` is the shorthand; `AppThemeBinding` gives you a `Default` fallback too.

---

**Key points:**

| Topic | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Follows `Application.RequestedTheme` (`Light` / `Dark` / `Unspecified`) |
| **Live updates** | Yes — toggles instantly when the user changes the OS theme |
| **Resource dictionaries** | For reuse, define colors in `App.xaml` and reference them with `StaticResource`/`DynamicResource` inside `AppThemeBinding` |
| **Force a theme** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark;` to override the system setting |

For larger apps, prefer defining themed colors in `App.xaml` resources and referencing them, rather than inlining hex values on every element.
